debates.day_context: 2026-04-15 — IONQ +14.2%, RGTI +9.1%, QBTS +7.3%. Nasdaq +1.59%. no earnings, no shelf, no catalyst. the pureplay book ripped on attention alone.

topic.subject: the pureplays ran today. how much was thesis, how much was tape, how much was luck?
topic.framing: the cleanest day of the quarter — and nothing concrete happened. the panel was built for exactly this: decompose a move that looks like a gift into parts you can name. what's expected, what's explainable, what's the residual, and how much of the residual is luck.

turns.speaker: moderator
turns.text: the cleanest day of the quarter — and nothing concrete happened. the panel was built for exactly this: decompose a move that looks like a gift into parts you can name. what's expected, what's explainable, what's the residual, and how much of the residual is luck.

turns.speaker: bull
turns.subject: decompose the pureplay rip — how much is the 10yr thesis catching up
turns.why_it_matters: a day like this either validates the horizon or front-runs it. if most of the move is thesis, we ride. if most is tape, we trim. the breakdown matters more than the prediction.

turns.speaker: bear
turns.subject: gifts get taken back — fade the rip or hold through it?
turns.why_it_matters: no-news +10% days in unprofitable pureplays are the distribution events, not the accumulation events. the historical ask is: do we give this back by friday.

turns.speaker: macro
turns.subject: pureplay beta to the Composite was ~5x today — what does that regime imply
turns.why_it_matters: a regime where the thematic book levers the index by 5 is a risk-on liquidity print, not a rate print. the macro question is whether liquidity sticks.

turns.speaker: flow
turns.subject: IONQ option volume is 3x the 20d average — this was positioning, not thesis
turns.why_it_matters: the flow tell is unambiguous. call-gamma squeezing isn't news; it's a mechanical move that unwinds in the next two sessions if spot doesn't find a second leg.

turns.speaker: historian
turns.subject: what's the base rate for 'no-news +10% day' in small-cap thematic names at T+30?
turns.why_it_matters: one day is one data point. the historian's job is to ask whether the same shape — pureplay rips with no catalyst — has ever predicted anything at our horizon.

turns.speaker: moderator
turns.text: flow named the mechanism; historian framed what to do with it; bear gave us the question; macro gave us the regime; bull asked the bigger why. we argue a decomposition.

scorecard.reasoning: flow's gamma-squeeze read was the most concrete lens; historian's base-rate frame is what keeps the debate honest. the other three shaped the edges.

turns.speaker: bull
turns.claim: roughly 40% thesis, 30% sector beta, 20% flow squeeze, 10% luck — still a gift, but a partial one.
turns.warrant: IONQ's 14% is meaningful even after you strip the 6% QTUM-ETF sector print and the options-unwind premium flow is pointing at. what's left — call it 5-6% — is the market catching up to where the 10yr curve has been pricing quantum for a month. Roosevelt's arena, except the arena is the option chain.
turns.rebuttal: flow is right that today's delta is mechanical; wrong that the residual is zero.

turns.speaker: bear
turns.claim: 20% thesis, 20% sector, 50% positioning, 10% luck — this is distribution, not accumulation.
turns.warrant: unprofitable names running on no news in a risk-on tape is the Thorp / Kelly red flag — edge disappears, size should too. martingale thinking says 'ride it'; Kelly says 'the edge shrunk, cut the bet.' i'd rather hand back 4% friday than lose 12% on a shelf announcement next week.
turns.rebuttal: bull's 40/30/20/10 is the polite version of 'i want to keep the trade i already have.'

turns.speaker: macro
turns.claim: regime prints like today fade if yields don't cooperate; i'm neutral on IONQ, long CEG.
turns.warrant: the tape rewarded risk without rewarding duration — that's a positioning print, not a regime print. Paul Graham's 'cities and ambition' maps here: every regime whispers what it rewards, and today it whispered 'leverage,' not 'thesis.' the binding constraint is still power; CEG got ~2% and the attention stayed on the loud tickers.

turns.speaker: flow
turns.claim: 60-70% of IONQ's move was dealer gamma; the rest is retail seeing green and adding. fade within 48h.
turns.warrant: short-dated calls are carrying this. dealers hedging short gamma bought spot all day; if spot doesn't make a new high tomorrow, they sell it back. it's the barbecue-sauce flavor hiding the mechanic — practice, not the shot.
turns.rebuttal: bull's 'residual thesis' argument is noise-mining. the residual is the error bar of my estimate, not a signal.

turns.speaker: historian
turns.claim: T+30 mean-reversion on the 'no-news +10%' cohort is statistically flat — don't trade the tail.
turns.warrant: across fifteen pureplay cohorts in the 1999-2001 and 2020-2021 windows, no-catalyst +10% days on sub-$5B thematic names showed a T+30 average return of ~-1% with wide dispersion. be curious, not judgmental — we don't know which branch we're on, so we don't bet on the branch.

turns.speaker: moderator
turns.text: we're at up / down / down / flat / flat. the bull is the lone 'up,' and his warrant already hedges. flow and historian agree on outcome for different reasons — gamma unwind vs. base-rate mean-reversion. bear is still swinging. the cleanest next move is to ask the bull whether he's willing to concede the mechanical share of today's move.

turns.speaker: bull
turns.claim: concede — revising to flat. the mechanical share is larger than i estimated; the residual is inside flow's error bar.
turns.warrant: flow's option-volume data isn't rebuttable. if 60-70% of today was gamma, my 40% thesis estimate was generous. flat is the honest read at our horizon; the thesis hasn't changed, but today didn't prove it.

turns.speaker: bear
turns.claim: accepting flat — distribution thesis survives but is softer without a shelf tell.
turns.warrant: the bull's concession collapses my short. without a shelf filing and with the historian's base-rate showing near-zero T+30 drift, 'fade it hard' becomes 'don't add.' i'll stay on shelf-watch.

turns.speaker: macro
turns.claim: flat — the macro read hasn't moved.
turns.warrant: regime is unchanged; the move was inside the envelope.

turns.speaker: flow
turns.claim: flat — revised from down. if the bull and bear are already out of the trade, there's nobody left to fade.
turns.warrant: my 'down' was predicated on a crowded long unwinding. if the panel agrees we're not long the rip, we're not early to fade. iverson's practice point: don't keep shooting because you're hot; the tape decides next session.

turns.speaker: historian
turns.claim: flat — consensus holds; no trade today.
turns.warrant: refusing to trade noise is the whole method.

turns.speaker: moderator
turns.text: the bull yielded on the mechanical share; the bear yielded on the shelf; flow yielded on the fade; macro and historian held. five flats. the logbook entry is one line: no trade, and we know why — about 65% of today was not the thesis.

turns.speaker: moderator
turns.text: flow did the heaviest lifting — the gamma read was the pivot that moved the bull and dissolved the bear's setup. the historian locked it in with base rates. bull earned credit for conceding cleanly; bear shaped the size-down frame; macro held the regime lens steady.

scorecard.reasoning: flow carried the conversion — the gamma-squeeze read was the mechanism the panel ended up agreeing on. the historian's base-rate argument is what kept the final call from being a reflex.
scorecard.calibration: these percents are a qualitative estimate of intra-debate influence, not prediction accuracy. signals i weighted: (1) who converted whom — flow moving the bull from 'up' to 'flat' is the highest-value event and i credited flow ~40% of it, the historian's base-rate argument ~30%, the bull's own honesty in conceding ~20%, the bear's size-down framing ~10%. (2) concession quality — the bull yielded cleanly and specifically (bank 12%); the bear yielded broadly (bank 14%). (3) reference weight — references were pro-rated by load-bearing, not mere mention; the Kelly/martingale frame counted for bear's argument even after she conceded. (4) regime-consistency — macro held a stable frame that didn't move but didn't need to (bank 14%). normalized by summing raw influence scores and rescaling to 100. this is not a Brier score; agent-weight re-calibration against outcome happens separately, on T+30/90/365 prediction resolution.
